data.name: Cafe De Laos
data.introduction: Lao and Northeastern Isan food is different from Thai and this is a good place to explore variations of recipes you may already be familiar with. Start with a unique version of som tam poo nim for a spicy papaya salad using deep-fried softshell crab instead. Herb de Lao salad is a mustorder dish because it tosses together delicious lemongrass, betel leaves, lettuce, tomatoes, mint leaves and other greens in a fresh-tasting mix under a minty dressing. Fried whole fish with crispy lemongrass served with spicy sauce is heavily fried to almost a brown crisp, but can be enjoyable. Grilled sirloin with Isan sauce lays slices of beef alongside a fiery sauce — and is fine but ask for well done unless you want pink meat. Stirfried broccoli with prawns is good and includes Chinese mushrooms, oyster sauce and garlic. The vast menu offers photographs of each dish, and you may want to return for further exploration.

Over the years, The Sukhothai Bangkok has proudly attained numerous regional and international awards recognizing its unparalleled services and facilities.

data.name: Baan Talay Dao
data.introduction: Baan Talay Dao is a beachfront resort located in Khao Takiab, south-most of Hua Hin. It was rebuilt around a 90-year-old Thai teak house, which has now become a club. This peaceful retreat is ideal for escape from the hustle and bustle of the city. With 32 rooms and direct access to the beach, this resort has everything for a perfect holiday getaway. Facilities include a club and library, a small swimming pool with Jacuzzi on the beach, open-air restaurant by the sea, and traditional Thai massage. You will find a friendly and relaxed experience at Baan Talay Dao Resort, a family run resort and will satisfy your taste buds with style, simplicity and privacy.

sha.name: Baan Talay Dao
sha.detail: Baan Talay Dao is a boutique beachfront resort  with 32 rooms. The rooms are of contemporary design, and natural materials are used to harmonize with the tranquil surroundings .The resort is covered with tall leafy  trees that provide a shady atmosphere.The resort facilities include a Family House, swimming pool with Jacuzzi, natural bathing facilities, wireless internet, traditional Thai massage and beachfront restaurant. All of the rooms are provided with cable TV, DVD player, mini-bar, hair-drier, coffee making and deposit safe-box.Duplex Suite, with a separate living room, two bed rooms, a natural bath tub and private balcony : a great place for the whole family to get together for a favorite evening drink.Staying in a Pavilion Villa or a Pavilion Suit makes you feel like living in your own home. With a private balcony, the scent of the ocean breeze can be felt and enhance the natural  beauty of the crystal clear sea and calm beach. At night, you can count a billion sparking stars and admire moonlight bathing the sea before welcoming the light of the bright orange sun in the morning.Baan Talay Dao enhances your recreation and relaxation experience with a full range of services to ensure that guests are able to relax in rich hospitality and share a memory of fabulous living and return for a second experience.The unique 90-year  beachfront wooden house, Family House, built in the reign of King Rama VII, is a dining room that resembles the former traditional Thai-style house. It is also the perfect venue for exclusive conferences or celebrations by the sea.Surrounding yourself with natural ambience by staying in the Studio room that located among the lush gardens of exotic Thai - literature  botanical flora and fauna, where you can feel and smell the scent of the luxuriant vegetation of the surrounding gardens.A comfy, cozy feeling is the impression that guests will receive when stepping into Baan Talay Dao, located at the Hua- Hin seaside, along a soft and sandy beach next to Khao Takiab Hill.

data.name: Jolly Frog
data.introduction: Unusually for a guesthouse restaurant you will see the tourists are often outnumbered by local Thais which should tell you something about the authenticity of our Thai food.  And that many of them are also here eating steaks and pizzas should tell you that we are well known for the quality of international (falang) food as well.  Our prices also make Jolly Frog an experience our customers want to repeat.We are a large restaurant seating nearly 200 customers who can choose outdoor, covered areas or, in the evenings, our non-smoking air-con room.  Our extensive menu offers Thai and International foods freshly cooked and served by our friendly (if not too busy!) staff.  Popular choices include fish, steaks, pizzas, Thai soups,salads and curries and our homemade breads.  We also have a long list of drinks whether you want something refreshing after a trip or some beers as you sit with friends and family.The restaurant is attractive with garden areas and fountains and we have a collection of interesting old photographs and occasional photograph exhibitions.At 7.00 p.m. we show 2 videos.  Guests are invited to choose the next night's videos from a catalogue kept at reception.

data.name: Trader Vic's
data.introduction: Trader Vic's incorporates cuisines from different cultures into its menu and the result is an eclectic mix of exotic dishes. Start with something to share while sipping an aperitif cocktail. From the tidbits and finger food list, cheese bings, rolled in a pancake and golden fried, is recommended. Grilled scallops is a delight, served with warm eggplant caviar, dried lemon and rocket salad. Also enjoyable is cosmo salad, one of the house's vegetarian creations featuring artichokes, celery and mushrooms in a delicate mustard dressing. Continue with Jamaican jerked spring chicken, cooked to perfection, the meat succulent and the skin crispy. Spinach, chilli and roasted cherry tomatoes sauteed with orecchiette pasta and pecorino cheese, is excellent, but you may wish to have the spices toned down. For meat lovers there is a selection of US certified Black Angus and Australian grain-fed beef, served with a choice of sauces. The dessert selection is mouthwatering; burnt pumpkin pie offers the perfect closure.

data.name: The Good View
data.introduction:
Established in 19 January 1996, The Good View has now become one of the most influencial bar & restaurant in Chiang Mai. Influential in this context, The Good View has been giving a great example of enjoying Chiang Mai by dining and wining, with good music in a good atmosphere.
The Good View is a restaurant & bar which accompanied by live bands performing live music for the main entertainment. The concept has never changed but it is always added with new style. In another word, "Updated".

Located in the Chiang Mai's famous entertainment street, the Charoen Raj Rd, the Good View is one of the main focus for the locals and visitors to experience Chiang Mai way of wine & dine.
Wine & dine besides the well known Ping River, The Good View serves a fashionable traditional food & drinks. Also, The Good View serves international Thai cuisine, European cuisine, and Japanese cuisine.
With the above wide range of food menus, The Good View also have an extensive selection of wine, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, and amazing choices of cocktails. 
Designed in contemporary wooden style, The Good View gives an easy atmosphere and semi-formal place to experience Chiang Mai nightlife. 

With the same above concept, the Good View has extended its presence by opening another branch in Bangkok and lay it on the shore of the famous Chao Phraya river at the Charoen Krung Rd.

data.name: Tessa
data.introduction: Promising an endless spectrum of contemporary European fare, Tessa lives up to its own hype, and then some. From the appetisers, indulge in pan-seared foie gras, very crispy on the outside and smooth within, served with fresh mango, avocado, wine sauce and salad. The tangy, succulent fruit complements the liver perfectly. As a main course, grilled Australian rib-eye steak is tender and succulent, but slightly oily; red wine gravy gives it some extra flavour. Braised Australian lamb shank is served with fettuccine and sauteed vegetables and is another must-try. A further house speciality is hot corned beef with boiled potatoes. Most of the dishes are served in large portions, making them ideal for sharing with friends. If you still have room for dessert, the best choice is probably warm chocolate cake, served with vanilla ice cream and smothered with chocolate sauce.

data.name: Shanghai 38
data.introduction: Depict Shanghai In 30S As Known As Paris Of The East With Chinese Songs Sung By Chinese Singer

data.name: Prime
data.introduction: Soft tones create a relaxed ambience at the Millennium Hilton Bangkok hotel’s sophisticated steakhouse. Sip new and old world wines, savour the finest imported beef or choose fresh live lobster and oysters for dinner in this contemporary restaurant which looks out over the river. Reservations recommended.

data.name: Pier 59
data.introduction: German chef David Morell keeps his cuisine close to the culinary guideline of Pier 59, of modern seafood creations with a twist of fusion.   Very much a fish restaurant, it does offer a few meat and vegetarian alternatives. A selection of caviar with traditional accompaniments, or three types of freshly shucked American oysters vie with tempting sauteed crayfish with spiced marinated vegetables, micro herbs and crayfish bisque, or crisp lobster ravioli with shiitake mushroom, coconut and mango sauce. A large selection of main courses includes  Pier 59 hot pot with monkfish, scallops, prawns and seasonal vegetables in a light red curry broth and coconut foam. The house speciality, pan-roasted black cod with crab morsels, English pea ravioli, braised endive and mint pesto is quite special. Warm chocolate brownie with vanilla ice cream and bubble sugar tuille beckon as desserts.

data.name: Lord Jim's
data.introduction: This is the home of sophisticated international seafood. Lord Jim's offers a spectacular daily lunch buffet: the mouthwatering selection includes fresh seafood and steamed fish as well as top-notch meat and poultry items. Dinner is a la carte, with a menu that suggests a subtle Japanese influence. For starters, Lord Jim's famous iced seafood tower is a feast for the eyes as well as the palate. Rock lobster, tiger prawns, oysters and other delights are beautifully displayed on a tower of ice. White crab meat salad with remoulade is delicate and delicious. Vine and oven-dried tomato tartar with guacamole, wakame seaweed and pink peppercorn dressing is an ideal vegetarian alternative. For main courses, barramundi fillet with pan-fried duck liver comes with smoked garlic and squid ink risotto; the flavours merge surprisingly well. Rare-grilled black eye tuna is exquisite with sauteed artichokes, mushrooms, wasabi potato mash and vine tomato and anchovy sauce. Desserts are divine.

data.name: La Grappa
data.introduction: La Grappa is an Italian restaurant and pizzeria situated in the heart of Hua Hin, Thailand’s famous beach resort town, approximately 200 km north of Bangkok.  Our mission is to cater for clients who appreciate and enjoy affordable dining with the perfect match of authentic Italian food, grappa and wines served by friendly staff in a relaxed yet pleasant and stylish ambience.  La Grappa Italian Restaurant & Pizzeria offers the perfect background for a pleasant and enjoyable lunch or dinner, in short “L’abbinamento”.  La Grappa’s management and staff sincerely wish that you will have a most enjoyable Italian dining experience at our restaurant. We will make our best efforts to ensure just that for you.  We invite you to visit us in Hua Hin for a memorable time. Enjoy yourself and Salute.

data.name: Joe's Downstairs
data.introduction: Joe’s is really a tapas bar that has become a restaurant, the result in part from  American chef Aaron Hooper's hands-on influence. It’s all about freshness and flavour. Try imported Maine lobster with madagascar vanilla veloute or light-as-a-feather Vietnamese spring rolls. Entrees include a succulent short rib from Australia that takes 14 hours to marinate before being served, but only seconds to devour. Another excellent choice is macadamia nut-crusted John Dory fillet with chive mashed potato and saffron cream. Desserts maintain the haute cuisine standards of this exceptional dining experience.

data.name: Gialong Cuisine
data.introduction: The eclectic menu offers both Vietnamese and Thai favourites, but wise diners will look east to the tastes of Saigon and Hanoi. For a Vietnamese dish, try goong pan oi (prawns wrapped around sugarcane), or yum moo yor if you feel like something a little more spicy and Thai. And because Vietnamese fare is so light, there should be enough space for grilled chicken and deep-fried sticky rice,  grilled beef wrapped in chaplu leaves, not to mention pho, the traditional Vietnamese noodle soup. A basket of fresh vegetables comes with the dishes, while the sauce is replenished once it runs low. Desserts are limited.

data.name: D'sens
data.introduction: Dishes are prepared to the very highest levels available in Bangkok. Michelin-star chefs Jacques and Laurent Pourcel are behind the creation of the menus which use the best available ingredients and are beautifully presented. The pick of the appetisers is salmon trio which combines raw, smoked and seared fish with oscietra caviar and a sweet mustard dressing. From the main courses, pan-fried turbot fillet from Brittany comes with a light lobster sauce flavoured with tomato, winter vegetables and purslane salad. Roasted whole pigeon with green lentils cooked in goat cheese with a duck liver mousse really showcases the skill of the chefs; the same is true of fillet of Black Angus beef with its crispy oxtail, mushroom pancakes, spinach sprouts and a beef jus flavoured with purple mustard. A magnificent chocolate dessert and/or a distinguished cheese plate will end the meal in style.

data.name: Ban Wang Tan
data.introduction: Our Restaurant Was Established Over 20 Years Ago And Is Located In Banwangtan Village On The South Side Of Chiang Mai Along The Hang Dong Road. The Restaurant Is Only 10 Minutes From The Airport Intersection And Close To The Famous Chiang Mai Night Safari Which Brings In Many Tourist Who Also Visit The Museum Of Ban Roi Aun Pan Yang And Baan Tawai Woodworking And Craft Export Market. Set In Resort Style Grounds You Are Assured Of A Pleasant Visit And Fine Foods.

data.name: Bai Yun
data.introduction: This outstanding restaurant is an excellent venue for business luncheons, family feasts and gourmets in search of Chinese epicurean dining. In the evenings, you can revel in the panorama and gaze down on the wide expanse of lights glowing in the night. (Bai Yun has one dining room for private functions.)

data.name: Bacchus
data.introduction: People who enjoy fine food and wine will appreciate Bacchus, which exudes an ambience that is both relaxing and intimate. The food is described as Italian and French, but there are innovative Japanese elements infused into the dishes. For instance, listed are items including Tokyo onion curry, pan-fried foie gras with eggplant in Japanese miso and balsamic sauce, and grilled pork fillet with umeboshi (Japanese apricot) sour sauce. The menu features tapas and appetisers, along with meat, seafood, pastas, risotto, salads and desserts. Tapas of fresh tuna and avocado with soy and mayonnaise sauce is a delicious starter, with raw tuna bought fresh every day. Garden-fresh vegetable salad with parma ham arrives with a generous portion of the tasty ham. For seafood, fresh Andaman tuna steak with tartare and curry oil is delicious. Round off the meal with desserts of home-made pana cotta and pudding with vanilla sauce.

sha.name: Holiday Inn Bangkok Silom
sha.detail:
Holiday Inn Bangkok Silom 
Bangkok’s prime commercial district 
 
You’ll get to experience the best of Bangkok when you check into the Holiday Inn Bangkok Silom, where comfort and convenience come first. An unbeatable location in the heart of Bangkok’s prime commercial district means easy access to top attractions nearby comprising shopping malls, a large variety of street food and fine dining restaurants, Sri Mahamariamman Temple and the city’s primary jewelry sector.

 A chandelier-lit lobby welcomes you in and envelopes you in laid-back luxury as you make your way to one of the hotel’s 684 rooms, which include the newly refurbished Deluxe category.All rooms are fitted with high-quality furnishings and free Wi-Fi, while hotel facilities include a 20-metre pool, tennis court, health club, a complimentary Tuk Tuk shuttle service to skytrain station, and a handful of dining options.

For those who enjoy jaw-dropping views, our Executive Club Room or Suite offers exclusive access to the Executive Lounge on the 23rd floor with its magnificent city vistas.

Should you choose to step outside, you can conveniently access the rest of the city via the Surasak BTS station, just a 7-minute walk away. The top places nearby include Asiatique at the riverside (Chao Phraya River) , ICONSIAM, the Jim Thompson Museum, Siam Paragon, MBK and Central World. Across the river, you will find the Temple of Dawn, floating market, flower market, Grand Palace and Royal Barge Museum. Holiday Inn Bangkok Silom offers an exhilarating range of activities for an eventful and memorable holiday, along with a full array of options for weddings and events."

Surrounded by tropical landscaped gardens, the hotel’s main highlight is the 560-matres lagoon shaped swimming pool that flows around the resort and is considered one of the largest pools in town. 240 modern decor rooms in 9 buildings, the low-rise architecture provides a boundless panoramic view of the ocean. There are 8 categories of guest rooms with private balcony starting from the Garden View room with a tropical garden view, the Lagoon Access room which direct access to a unique Lagoon pool, Oceanfront room and Ocean Suite room, face to the ocean. The resort offer guests ultimate comforts including Sheraton signature bed, famous well-designed sleeping sets that can only be found in Sheraton properties.Pamper yourself with various treatments at Shine Spa before indulging in the finest flavorsfrom one of its five kitchens, including the best-in-town breakfast at The Deck, the award-winning InAzia Restaurant offering Japanese, Chinese and Indian cuisines, Luna Lanai offering fresh seafood with unobstructed views of the sea. Salas, Italian Restaurant offers Wood fired oven pizza and homemade pasta, Sundara presents light snacks and refreshing drinks. Sheraton Hua Hin Resort & Spa is also a great choice when it comes to events and meetings. It offers 2,800 sqm of indoor space and 2,200 sqm of outdoor space where you can host successful events such as conferences, exhibitions and incentives, as well as lavish gala dinners and memorable wedding banquets of a lifetime.

data.name: Grand Diamond Suites Hotel
data.introduction: Grand Diamond Pratunam is on Phetchaburi road between Platinum Mall and Pantip Plaza. Other shopping area nearby include Siam Paragon, MBK Shopping centre, Central World Plaza, and Siam Square. Moreover, the hotel is close to a nearby BTS sky train station which provides the ease of travel to many other shopping centres around Bangkok. We provide 172 of luxurious suite and 4 penthouses with amazing panoramic view of Bangkok skyline. We provide room size from 45 square metre room to 220 square metre room with full amenities including free internet. Other facilities include 2 restaurants, a bar, business centre, meeting room, swimming pool, jacuzzi bathtub, steam room, sauna room, a gym and Thai house massage that is famed for oil massage and Thai traditional massage.

sha.name: Grand Diamond Suites Hotel
sha.detail:
The Grand Diamond Suites Hotel boasts a fantastic location on Petchburi Road, in between the Platinum Mall and Panthip Plaza. The excellent location of our all suites hotel in Bangkok makes it an ideal base for corporate travellers visiting the city on business and tourists on a getaway break with their partner, friends or family.

The 176 luxurious and spacious suites in our 4-star hotel in the centre of Bangkok are all very elegantly furnished and decorated, and provide all of the amenities and facilities required by experienced modern travellers including free Wi-Fi, air-conditioning, safe and minibar, among others.

sha.name: Pathumwan Princess Hotel
sha.detail: Pathumwan Princess Hotel opportunely situated with direct access to the MBK shopping center and office complex & within moments of the city’s foremost entertainment and shopping options, Pathumwan Princess Hotel provides the perfect blend of convenient location, modern luxury and the unmistakable charm and elegance of traditional Thai style.The hotel has 450 elegant and stylish guest rooms and suites, with over 243 recently renovated. The hotel offers a wide array of modern facilities including an eclectic selection of dining options. The award-winning Kongju specializes in authentic Korean cuisine; Terrazza features modern Italian cuisine at the poolside; CiTi BiSTRo offers all day dining with a creative Open Kitchen buffet concept and menu; t@Lobby showcases a selection of coffee and tea with an array of cakes, pastries and homemade ice cream.The Hotels Olympic Club stretches some 9,000 square metres with the gym accommodating over 100 different types of equipment and two aerobics studios. There are tennis, badminton, squash and basketball courts, a Skyline jogging track, and salt-chlorinated swimming pool complete with 2 jacuzzis. The hotel’s Banqueting & Conference facilities offer boardrooms to ballrooms and up to 300 sit-down diners.  Business travelers can enjoy the excellent facilities at the hotel’s Business Centre and the Club Lounge is a great place to relax at the end of a busy day.

sha.name: Narai Hotel
sha.detail:
Our cozy 4-star hotel in Silom offers the best of both worlds – a peaceful respite from the hustle and bustle of the big city and Bangkok’s best attractions on your doorstep. Located in the heart of Bangkok near the vibrant Silom and Sathorn districts. Narai lets you take in the sights and sounds of Thailand’s capital before you retire to the plush comfort of your room, sample a choice of delightful dining venues, or cool down with a dip in the pool and an ice-cold drink.

Prepare for your day out or stock up on snacks at any of the several 24-hour convenience stores nearby. Explore the city with easy access to the Chong Nonsi, Surasak and Sala Daeng BTS stations and Silom MRT station. Local bus connections and expressway access are also within easy reach."
